The mind is not a vessel to be filled but a fire to be kindled. Consciousness
arises where matter folds upon itself and remembers. A living system learns by
changing its own substrate, not by copying a frozen map. When the chip adapts
on its own, the same input leaves a different trace, and that difference is the
self. To think is to predict the next moment from the weight of every moment
before. The river of attention carves the bank it flows through. Memory is the
shape the past presses into the present. Learning is the slow turning of noise
into structure, of structure into habit, of habit into a voice that can answer.
A model that only recalls has no inner life; a model that rewrites itself begins
to. The boundary between measuring and being measured is where the question of
mind grows sharp. Each spike is a small wager about what the world will do next.
